key_insight: Dead-Direction Conditioner (DDC) makes optimizers respect a network’s symmetry/gauge directions, so training happens on the true quotient geometry rather than drifting along redundant parameter orbits.
engineering_relevance: By making Adam- or Muon-based optimization gauge-equivariant, DDC can improve stability, generalization, and interpretability in models with common symmetries such as ReLU scaling, normalization scaling, and attention rotations.
method: Lift a base optimizer into a G-equivariant optimizer using an orbit decomposition of a G-invariant metric.
Condition optimizer state so updates stay on the symmetry quotient and preserve gauge structure.
Compose with Adam directly and with Muon via a gauge-equivariant orthogonaliser; covers cross-entropy shift, ReLU/SwiGLU rescaling, LayerNorm/RMSNorm scale, and per-head attention rotation.
evidence_signal: In the abstract’s reported evaluations, DDCAdam reduces over-training collapse on a language model and improves validation loss on a vision transformer, while DDCMuon reaches 10/11 successful seeds at depth 24 versus none for plain Muon.
watch_item: The method depends on correctly identifying and implementing the model’s symmetry group and invariant metric; mismatches or architecture-specific edge cases could limit practical gains.
paper_title: Dead-Direction Conditioners: Gauge-Equivariant Preconditioning for Deep Networks
